articles.title: Gold languishes near daily low as geopolitical risks and hawkish Fed bets lift USD
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) maintains its offered tone through the first half of the European session and remains vulnerable below the $4,580 horizontal resistance, retested earlier this Tuesday.

articles.title: Iran's IRGC identifies hostile aircrafts entering its airspace, downs MQ9 drone
articles.expand: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) announced it had detected and shot down an MQ-9 drone. The IRGC said in a statement that it has the right to respond to any US ceasefire breach. The drone shootdown followed a maritime clash tonight. US Central Command (CENTCOM) confirmed that American forces sank two IRGC mine-laying speedboats in th...

articles.title: Euro: ECB hawkishness fails to lift versus US Dollar – Societe Generale
articles.expand: The report by Societe Generale notes that Eurozone yields are slightly firmer after hawkish comments from ECB member Schnabel, who highlights rising medium-term inflation expectations and signals a likely rate hike in June.

articles.title: Chart alert: AUD/NZD rally set to continue after hitting 13-year high
articles.expand: AUD/NZD may extend its rally after reaching a fresh 13-year high ahead of the RBNZ policy decision. While the RBNZ is expected to deliver a hawkish hold at 2.25%, widening Australia-New Zealand bond yield spreads and the RBA’s more aggressive tightening path continue to favour the Australian dollar. Technical analysis highlights strong bullish mome...

articles.title: Indian Rupee corrects as oil prices rebound due to renewed Mideast uncertainty
articles.expand: The Indian Rupee’s (INR) rally against the US Dollar (USD) hits a pause after a four-day winning streak on Tuesday.

articles.title: USD/JPY Price Forecast: Dollar ticks up to 159.00 as Iran’s peace talks wobble
articles.expand: The US Dollar (USD) appreciates against the Japanese Yen (JPY) for the second consecutive day on Tuesday, hitting session highs above 159.00 at the time of writing, as fresh US attacks on Iran put the peace process into question.

articles.title: Euro strengthens against British Pound as ECB signals June hike
articles.expand: The EUR/GBP cross gathers strength to near 0.8630 during the early European session on Tuesday. Hawkish remarks from the ‌European Central Bank (ECB) provide some support to the Euro (EUR) against the British Pound (GBP).

articles.title: Fed: Hawkish risks for rates – BNY
articles.expand: BNY’s John Velis and David Tam note that recent FOMC minutes and comments from Fed Governor Waller point to higher two‑way risk for US interest rates.

articles.title: New Zealand Dollar: RBNZ set to wait and hope – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Volkmar Baur at Commerzbank expects the Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ) to keep its policy rate unchanged, maintaining a wait‑and‑see stance unlike the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA).

articles.title: Markets reserve some caution on US-Iran developments
articles.expand: The war continues to rage on but it looks like we're on the cusp of some form of agreement or memorandum of understanding (MOU) being signed off between both sides. While on the surface it is billed as "a deal to end the conflict", let's be reminded of what the stakes are here. This framework agreement or MOU is mainly to set out new terms for how ...

articles.title: What are the main events for today?
articles.expand: EUROPEAN SESSION In the European session, we don't have much on the agenda other than a couple of low tier releases like the Spanish PPI and the UK CBI which are not going to change anything for the respective central banks. The ECB is widely expected to hike in June and then wait at least until September to deliver another rate hike if necessary. ...

articles.title: ECB’s Schnabel Says Iran Peace Deal May Already Be Too Late to Avoid June Hike
articles.expand: Even a successful US-Iran peace agreement may no longer be enough to stop the European Central Bank from tightening policy next month. ECB Executive Board member Isabel Schnabel warned today that the energy shock unleashed by the conflict has already gone too far, arguing that inflation pressures are now spreading through the wider Eurozone economy...

articles.title: Canadian Dollar steadies as safe-haven demand offsets higher Oil prices
articles.expand: USD/CAD inches higher after registering minor losses in the previous day, trading around 1.3810 during the Asian hours on Tuesday. The commodity-linked Canadian Dollar (CAD) is struggling against the US Dollar (USD), as rising risk aversion offsets any gains from higher crude oil prices.

articles.title: Australian Dollar loses ground against Japanese Yen on Middle East uncertainty
articles.expand: The AUD/JPY cross loses momentum to near 113.85 during the early European session on Tuesday. The Australian Dollar (AUD) weakens against the Japanese Yen (JPY) as Australia’s labor market surprised to the downside, and markets remain concerned over the Middle East uncertainty.

articles.title: "Looking through the inflation spike is no longer an option": ECB’s Schnabel hints at June hike
articles.expand: European Central Bank (ECB) Executive Board Member Isabel Schnabel said on Tuesday that, even if the Iran war ended today, policy action is needed given the damage to the energy infrastructure.

articles.title: Pound Sterling Price News and Forecast: GBP/USD erodes a part of Monday’s gains
articles.expand: The GBP/USD pair struggles to capitalize on the previous day's strong move up to levels beyond the 1.3500 psychological mark, or a one-and-a-half-week high, and attracts some sellers during the Asian session on Tuesday.

articles.title: Japan’s core CPI, excluding special factors, climbs 2.8% in April vs. 2.5% rise in March
articles.expand: The Bank of Japan (BoJ) said on Tuesday that Japan's core consumer inflation rate excluding one-off factors, as measured by the Japanese central bank's new gauge, reached 2.8% in April. This figure exceeded the BoJ’s 2% target and accelerated from 2.5% in March.

articles.expand: A split emerged within the NZIER Monetary Shadow Board ahead of this week’s Reserve Bank of New Zealand policy decision. While the majority recommended keeping the Official Cash Rate unchanged at 2.25%, several members argued that tightening should begin immediately as inflation pressures build. articles.expand: ECB President Christine Lagarde opened the door wider to a June rate hike after signaling that the central bank’s inflation forecasts are likely to move higher again. articles.expand: ECB president Christine Lagarde says the bank's March inflation forecast of 2.6% for 2025 will probably be revised upward when policymakers meet in June, confirming signals from other council members. articles.expand: Most NZIER shadow board members back holding New Zealand's OCR at 2.25% in May (statement due at pm New Zealand time on May 27), citing weak growth and Iran war uncertainty, though all agree rates must rise over the coming year. articles.title: ECB between fighting inflation and weaker growth
articles.expand: Analysis of the ECB’s dilemma as persistent inflation in the eurozone’s largest economies raises the possibility of a June rate hike despite weaker growth prospects.

articles.title: USDCAD stretches above the 200 day MA.

articles.title: EUR/USD Weekly Forecast: Awaiting more war-related headlines and central banks
articles.expand: The EUR/USD pair fell for a second consecutive week, settling not far above a fresh multi-week low of 1.1576. War-related headlines kept driving financial markets, coupled with mounting speculation that the United States (US) Federal Reserve (Fed) will deliver a rate hike before the year is over.

articles.title: GBP/USD Weekly Forecast: British Pound’s struggle with 200-day SMA set to extend
articles.expand: The British Pound (GBP) stalled its recovery from six-week lows near 1.3300 against the US Dollar (USD), as sellers continued to lurk above the 1.3450 region.

articles.expand: Canada retail sales rose a stronger-than-expected 0.9% mom in March to CAD 72.7B, beating expectations of a 0.5% increase. The headline gain was driven largely by gasoline stations and fuel vendors, where sales surged 12.4% during the month as higher oil prices linked to the Middle East conflict pushed fuel costs sharply higher.
